meta.release_notes:
# Changelog
All notable changes to this model will be documented in this file.

## [Unreleased]

## [2.0.0] - 2024-02-15
This model is in status draft, as this is just the conversion of existing model from bamm to samm and not released model.
This version can still be considered for updates.

## [1.0.0] - 2022-06-21
### Added
- initial model


### Changed
n/a

### Removed

nodes.label: IdConversion
nodes.description: The IdConversion aspect deals with the Conversion of (vehicle, global) Identifiers like VINs into Pseudonyms which are located in an OEM domain (VANs, UUIDs and other concepts) and vice versa. As there are multiple options for representing Identifiers and performing Pseudonymization, this model uses a combination of unqualified values for identifiers paired with namespaces (domains, keys).

nodes.label: ConvertOperation
nodes.description: The Convert operation converts from Identifiers into Pseudonyms of the respective domains. It is transactional in a sense that either all given identifiers could be converted or none (in which case the output will be empty). In all other cases, the converted identifier at index i of the output corresponds to the original identifier at index i of the input.

nodes.label: ReidentificationOperation
nodes.description: The Reidentification operation converts from Pseudonyms back into Identifiers of the respective domains. It is transactional in a sense that either all given identifiers could be converted or none (in which case the output will be empty). In all other cases, the converted identifier at index i of the output corresponds to the original identifier at index i of the input.

nodes.label: targetDomain
nodes.description: The Target Domain of a Conversion is an input parameter which qualifies the desired target pseudonyms/identifiers. In cases where the Conversion has only a single target domain and this is crystal-clear from the context, it may be ommited.

nodes.label: sourceDomain
nodes.description: The Source Domain of a Reidentification is an input parameter which qualifies the desired target identifiers. In cases where the Reidentification has only a single source domain and this is crystal-clear from the context, it may be ommited.

nodes.label: conversionInput
nodes.description: The Conversion Input lists the (qualified) source identifiers or pseudonyms which should be covered by the Convert operation.

nodes.label: conversionOutput
nodes.description: The Conversion Output lists the (qualified) target identifiers or pseudonyms which have been computed by the Convert operation.

nodes.label: key
nodes.description: The key hints to the type (identifier of a specific origin, pseudonym of a given domain).

nodes.label: value
nodes.description: The value property is either a local identifier or pseudonym. The format and type of the value depends on the key property.

nodes.label: values
nodes.description: The Values property hosts a list of (unqualified) identifiers or pseudonyms. The format and type of the value depends on the key property.

nodes.label: QualifiedBatchOfIdentifiersEither
nodes.description: A Qualified Batch of Identifiers (and Pseudonyms) has two representations. Either it lists a set of unqualified Identifiers under a common domain/key. Or it lists a set of individually qualified Identifiers.

nodes.label: QualifiedIdentifiersList
nodes.description: In a List of Qualified Identifiers, any Identifier can belong to its own domain.

nodes.label: IdList
nodes.description: A list of (unqualified) identifiers. This characteristic should be constrained in concrete implementations and in correspondence with associated domains of the Convert operation.

nodes.label: QualifiedListOfIdentifiersSingleEntity
nodes.description: In a Qualified List of (unqualified) Identifiers, any Identifier belongs to the same domain.

nodes.label: QualifiedListOfIdentifiersEntity
nodes.description: Combines a domain with a list of unqualified Identifiers

nodes.label: QualifiedIdentifierEntity
nodes.description: Qualified Identifier is an entity which combines a namespace (key) and a local name (value) component.

nodes.label: UrnCharacteristic
nodes.description: A Unique Resource Name (URN) preferrably in IRI syntax (with a trailing #)

nodes.label: IdCharacteristic
nodes.description: An (unqualified) Identifier. This characteristic should be constrained in concrete implementations and in correspondence with associated domains of the Convert operation.
